title: Fix backend restart silently failing when process started from different directory
description:
## Problem

The `make backend-restart` command silently fails when a backend server is already running but was started from a different working directory (or manually). The Makefile tracks PIDs in `.backend-pid`, but if the process was started elsewhere, the PID file doesn't exist or points to a dead process.

### Symptoms
- `make backend-restart` reports 'Backend server started (PID: XXXXX)'
- But the new process immediately exits because port 8080 is already bound
- Old backend continues running with stale code
- User thinks restart succeeded but changes aren't loaded

### Root Cause
- `start-server.sh` starts the backend with `nohup` in background
- If port 8080 is already bound, the new process fails silently
- The PID file gets updated with the new (now dead) PID
- No error is shown to the user

### Solution
1. Check if port 8080 is already bound before starting
2. If bound, fail with clear error message: 'Port 8080 already in use. Run make backend-stop-all first.'
3. Alternatively, add port check to `backend-run` target in Makefile

### Workaround
Use `pkill -f 'voice-code.server'` or `make backend-stop-all` before `make backend-run`
close_reason: Fixed in commit f1f863e - start-server.sh now detects port conflicts before starting and reads port from config.edn

title: Extract and validate provider from prompt message in server.clj
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/plans/2025-01-31-multi-provider-session-creation-design.md#backend-serverclj

## Context
The backend currently passes `nil` for explicit provider to `resolve-provider`. This task adds extraction of the `provider` field from new session prompts, validates it, and passes it to provider resolution.

## Requirements
- [ ] Extract `provider` field from prompt message data
- [ ] Only use provider for new sessions (when `new-session-id` present)
- [ ] Validate provider against `providers/known-providers`
- [ ] Return error for invalid provider values
- [ ] Silently ignore provider field on resumed sessions
- [ ] Pass explicit provider to `resolve-provider`
- [ ] Add provider to log statements

## Technical Approach
Update prompt handling in `handle-message`:

```clojure
(let [explicit-provider-str (:provider data)
      explicit-provider (when (and new-session-id explicit-provider-str)
                          (let [p (keyword explicit-provider-str)]
                            (when-not (contains? providers/known-providers p)
                              (throw (ex-info "Invalid provider"
                                              {:provider explicit-provider-str
                                               :valid (mapv name providers/known-providers)})))
                            p))
      session-metadata (when resume-session-id
                         (repl/get-session-metadata resume-session-id))
      provider (providers/resolve-provider explicit-provider session-metadata)
      ...]
```

- Files to modify: `backend/src/voice_code/server.clj`
- New files to create: None
- Dependencies: Task 1 (Copilot model override) should be done first

## Verification
- [ ] Unit test: Provider extracted from new session prompts
- [ ] Unit test: Provider ignored on resumed session prompts  
- [ ] Unit test: Invalid provider returns error with valid options list
- [ ] Unit test: Valid provider passed to resolve-provider
- [ ] Manual: Send prompt with `provider: "copilot"` and verify routing

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] server.clj: Extract `provider` from prompt message for new sessions
- [ ] server.clj: Validate provider against `providers/known-providers`
- [ ] server.clj: Pass explicit provider to `resolve-provider`

title: Worktree beads isolation via BEADS_DB environment variable
description:
## Design Document
@docs/design/worktree-beads-env-isolation.md

## Overview
Implement transparent, automatic isolation of beads databases per git worktree using the BEADS_DB environment variable. Each worktree gets a local .beads-local/ database that is automatically created on session start, eliminating noise from unrelated issues in the main repository.

## Key Components
1. New `voice-code.env` namespace with detection, initialization, and env building functions
2. Integration with `claude.clj` process execution
3. Integration with `commands.clj` process spawning
4. Worktree session creation in `server.clj`
5. Unit and integration tests
6. `.gitignore` update

## Acceptance Criteria
1. [ ] Worktrees are automatically detected by checking `git rev-parse --git-dir` output for `/worktrees/`
2. [ ] Detection results are cached per-directory to avoid repeated git calls
3. [ ] `.beads-local/local.db` is created when worktree session starts
4. [ ] `BEADS_DB` environment variable is set for all processes spawned in worktree directories
5. [ ] `bd` commands in worktrees see only worktree-specific issues
6. [ ] Main repository beads database is unaffected
7. [ ] `.beads-local/` is in `.gitignore`
8. [ ] All existing tests continue to pass
9. [ ] New unit tests cover detection, env building, and db initialization
close_reason: All implementation complete. Created voice-code.env namespace, integrated with claude.clj and commands.clj, updated worktree session creation to use local beads db, added .gitignore entry. All 281 backend tests and 12 iOS tests pass.

title: Add environment variable support to commands.clj process spawning
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/design/worktree-beads-env-isolation.md#integration-points

## Context
This task integrates the env namespace with shell command execution. When commands are run via the iOS Commands UI in a worktree, the BEADS_DB environment variable must be set so that `bd.ready`, `bd.list`, etc. use the local database.

## Requirements
- [ ] Modify `spawn-process` to call `env/env-for-directory`
- [ ] Use `ProcessBuilder.environment().putAll()` to add env vars
- [ ] Add require for `voice-code.env` namespace

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify: `backend/src/voice_code/commands.clj`
- Dependencies: Task "Create voice-code.env namespace" must complete first

Key changes:
```clojure
(let [env-vars (env/env-for-directory working-directory)
      pb (ProcessBuilder. ["bash" "-c" shell-command])
      _ (.directory pb (java.io.File. working-directory))
      _ (when (seq env-vars)
          (.putAll (.environment pb) env-vars))
      process (.start pb)]
  ...)
```

Note: ProcessBuilder.environment() returns a mutable map that inherits from parent process, so `.putAll` adds to (not replaces) the environment.

## Verification
- [ ] Existing commands.clj tests still pass
- [ ] Integration test: spawn-process in worktree passes BEADS_DB
- [ ] Manual: Run `bd.ready` command in worktree via iOS, verify sees local db

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC4: `BEADS_DB` environment variable is set for all processes spawned in worktree directories

## Parallelization
Can be worked alongside: "Add environment variable support to claude.clj"
close_reason: Added env require and modified spawn-process to call env/env-for-directory and pass vars via ProcessBuilder.environment().putAll(). All 9 available-commands tests pass.

title: Initialize beads local database in worktree session creation
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/design/worktree-beads-env-isolation.md#integration-points

## Context
This task ensures that when a new worktree session is created, the local beads database is initialized before any Claude prompts are sent. This is the setup step that makes the BEADS_DB environment variable useful.

## Requirements
- [ ] Call `env/ensure-beads-local!` after successful `create-worktree!`
- [ ] Handle failure case: send error to client if beads init fails
- [ ] Add require for `voice-code.env` namespace

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify: `backend/src/voice_code/server.clj`
- Dependencies: Task "Create voice-code.env namespace" must complete first

Key changes in worktree session creation flow:
```clojure
(let [wt-result (worktree/create-worktree! parent-directory branch-name worktree-path)]
  (if (:success wt-result)
    (let [beads-result (env/ensure-beads-local! worktree-path sanitized-name)]
      (if (:success beads-result)
        ;; Continue with Claude invocation...
        (let [prompt (worktree/format-worktree-prompt ...)] ...)
        ;; Beads init failed
        (send-error! channel {:error (:error beads-result) ...})))
    ;; Worktree creation failed
    ...))
```

## Verification
- [ ] Unit test: worktree creation calls ensure-beads-local!
- [ ] Unit test: beads init failure sends error to client
- [ ] Manual: Create worktree via iOS, verify `.beads-local/local.db` exists

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC3: `.beads-local/local.db` is created when worktree session starts

## Parallelization
Can be worked alongside: "Add environment variable support to claude.clj" and "Add environment variable support to commands.clj"
close_reason: Replaced worktree/init-beads! with env/ensure-beads-local! in server.clj worktree session creation. All 66 server tests pass.

title: Phase 7: Smart default provider selection
description:
## Problem

The current implementation hardcodes `:claude` as the default provider (Issue 6 in design review). This causes problems when:
- User only has Copilot installed but not Claude
- User has neither installed (should give clear error)

## Solution

Implement smart default provider selection as recommended in the design review:
```clojure
(defn get-default-provider []
  (let [installed (detect-installed-providers)]
    (cond
      ;; Only one provider installed - use it
      (= 1 (count installed)) (first installed)
      ;; Prefer Claude if available (backward compatible)
      (contains? (set installed) :claude) :claude
      ;; Fall back to first available
      :else (first installed))))
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Smart default selection prefers Claude when available (backward compatible)
- [ ] Falls back to Copilot if only Copilot is installed
- [ ] Returns nil if no providers installed (allows for clear error message)
- [ ] Tests cover all scenarios
- [ ] All existing tests pass

title: Multi-Provider Session Creation
description:
## Design Document
@docs/plans/2025-01-31-multi-provider-session-creation-design.md

## Overview
Enable iOS frontend to create sessions with provider of choice (Claude or Copilot) and continue sessions seamlessly using stored provider metadata.

## Goals
1. Allow users to select provider when creating a new session
2. Automatically continue sessions with the same provider they started with
3. Gracefully handle provider-specific feature differences (e.g., system prompts)

## Acceptance Criteria

### Backend
- [ ] providers.clj: Add `--model gpt-5-mini` to `build-cli-command :copilot`
- [ ] server.clj: Extract `provider` from prompt message for new sessions
- [ ] server.clj: Validate provider against `providers/known-providers`
- [ ] server.clj: Pass explicit provider to `resolve-provider`
- [ ] Unit tests: Provider resolution logic (explicit > metadata > default)
- [ ] Unit tests: Protocol handling (extract, ignore on resume, validate)
- [ ] Unit tests: CLI command building (flags per provider)

### iOS
- [ ] AppSettings.swift: Add `defaultProvider` property with UserDefaults persistence
- [ ] SettingsView.swift: Add provider picker section
- [ ] SettingsView.swift: Update system prompt footer to say "when applicable"
- [ ] ConversationView.swift: Add segmented provider picker for new sessions
- [ ] ConversationView.swift: Include `provider` field in prompt message for new sessions

### Documentation
- [ ] STANDARDS.md: Document `provider` field in WebSocket protocol prompt message

## Testing Constraints
All tests must mock provider CLI calls. No CLI invocation in tests.

## Deployment Order
Backend first, then iOS. Change is backward compatible in both directions.

title: Add provider selection to iOS ConversationView
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/plans/2025-01-31-multi-provider-session-creation-design.md#ios-new-session-flow

## Context
When creating a new session, users need to see and optionally change the provider before sending their first prompt. The picker should disappear after the first message.

## Requirements
- [ ] Add `@State private var selectedProvider: String` 
- [ ] Initialize from `settings.defaultProvider`
- [ ] Show segmented picker when `session.backendName == nil`
- [ ] Hide picker after first prompt sent
- [ ] Include `provider` field in prompt message for new sessions
- [ ] Persist selection to `session.provider`

## Technical Approach
```swift
// State
@State private var selectedProvider: String

// Initialize in init or onAppear
selectedProvider = settings.defaultProvider

// UI - above text input
if session.backendName == nil {
    Picker("Provider", selection: $selectedProvider) {
        Text("Claude").tag("claude")
        Text("Copilot").tag("copilot")
    }
    .pickerStyle(.segmented)
    .padding(.horizontal)
}

// In sendPromptText()
if isNewSession {
    message["provider"] = selectedProvider
    message["new_session_id"] = newSessionId
}
```

- Files to modify: `ios/VoiceCode/Views/ConversationView.swift`
- New files to create: None

title: macOS Desktop Redesign
description:
## Design Document
@docs/design/macos-desktop-redesign.md

## Overview
Transform the macOS app from 'iOS app on Mac' into a native desktop experience. Implements persistent sidebar navigation, push-to-talk voice input, command palette, proper Settings scene, menu bar quick access, and information density adjustments while maintaining voice-first identity.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Sidebar persists while viewing conversation (NavigationSplitView)
2. Push-to-talk works with Option+Space anywhere in app
3. Command palette opens with Cmd+K and supports fuzzy search
4. Settings opens as separate window via Cmd+,
5. Menu bar icon shows connection status and enables quick voice capture
6. Information density is higher than iOS (smaller spacing, more visible content)
7. All keyboard shortcuts documented in menu items
8. iOS functionality remains unchanged (no regressions)
9. Session switching possible without losing conversation context
10. Voice input works seamlessly via both click and keyboard

## Implementation Phases
- Phase 1: Navigation Foundation (NavigationSplitView, SessionSidebarView)
- Phase 2: Settings & Keyboard (Settings scene, .commands{} shortcuts)
- Phase 3: Push-to-Talk (PushToTalkModifier, RecordingIndicator)
- Phase 4: Command Palette (CommandPaletteView)
- Phase 5: Menu Bar Extra (MenuBarContentView, sendQuickPrompt)
- Phase 6: Polish (DesktopDensity, hover states)

title: Add sendQuickPrompt method to VoiceCodeClient
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/design/macos-desktop-redesign.md#data-model-changes

## Context
The menu bar quick capture needs a simplified API to send prompts without managing full session lifecycle. This method creates a temporary session, sends the prompt, and returns the response.

## Requirements
- [ ] Add sendQuickPrompt(text:directory:completion:) method
- [ ] Create or reuse session in specified directory
- [ ] Send prompt via existing WebSocket infrastructure
- [ ] Return first response text via completion handler
- [ ] Handle errors by returning nil
- [ ] Track directory usage via AppSettings.trackDirectoryUsage

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify: ios/VoiceCode/VoiceCodeClient.swift
- Dependencies: AppSettings.trackDirectoryUsage

Method signature:
```swift
func sendQuickPrompt(text: String, directory: String, completion: @escaping (String?) -> Void)
```

Implementation approach:
1. Find or create session with matching workingDirectory
2. Set up one-time response handler
3. Send prompt message
4. On response, call completion with text
5. On error/timeout, call completion with nil
6. Call settings.trackDirectoryUsage(directory)

## Verification
- [ ] Unit test: completion called with response text on success
- [ ] Unit test: completion called with nil on error
- [ ] Integration test: prompt sent and response received
- [ ] Manual: Menu bar Send button triggers prompt and shows response

## Acceptance Criteria
- Menu bar icon shows connection status and enables quick voice capture (API support)

## Parallelization
Depends on: AppSettings.trackDirectoryUsage
Required by: MenuBarExtra (for sendPrompt functionality)
close_reason: Implemented sendQuickPrompt method with completion handler, timeout handling, directory tracking, and 6 passing tests

title: Create DesktopDensity utilities for information density
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/design/macos-desktop-redesign.md#6-information-density-adjustments

## Context
Desktop screens can display more information than mobile. This utility provides platform-specific constants and view modifiers for tighter spacing, smaller fonts, and higher truncation thresholds.

## Requirements
- [ ] Create DesktopDensity.swift with static constants struct
- [ ] Define spacing constants (listRowVerticalPadding: 4, listRowHorizontalPadding: 8)
- [ ] Define typography constants (bodyFont: 13pt, captionFont: 11pt)
- [ ] Define truncation threshold (2000 chars vs 500 on iOS)
- [ ] Define sizing constants (minRowHeight: 28, iconSize: 14)
- [ ] Create .desktopDensity() view modifier for font
- [ ] Create .desktopListRow() view modifier for padding
- [ ] Platform-guard with #if os(macOS)

## Technical Approach
- Files to create: ios/VoiceCode/Utils/DesktopDensity.swift
- Files to modify: Various view files to apply modifiers
- Dependencies: None (can be done independently)

View modifiers pattern:
```swift
extension View {
    @ViewBuilder
    func desktopDensity() -> some View {
        #if os(macOS)
        self.font(DesktopDensity.bodyFont)
        #else
        self
        #endif
    }
}
```

Apply to: SessionSidebarRow, MessageView (truncation), list rows

## Verification
- [ ] Manual: macOS rows are visually denser than iOS
- [ ] Manual: Font sizes are smaller but readable
- [ ] Manual: Long messages truncate at 2000 chars on macOS
- [ ] Visual comparison: Side-by-side iOS vs macOS screenshots

## Acceptance Criteria
- Information density is higher than iOS (smaller spacing, more visible content)

## Parallelization
Can be worked alongside: Any task after Phase 1
Should be applied after: SessionSidebarView, CommandPaletteView

title: Register voicecode:// URL scheme for deep linking
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/design/macos-desktop-redesign.md#5-menu-bar-quick-access

## Context
The menu bar uses voicecode://session/{id} URLs to open specific sessions in the main window. This requires registering the URL scheme in Info.plist and handling incoming URLs.

## Requirements
- [ ] Add CFBundleURLTypes to Info.plist with voicecode scheme

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify: ios/VoiceCode/Info.plist, ios/VoiceCode/VoiceCodeApp.swift
- Dependencies: MacOSRootView (for session navigation)

Info.plist addition:
```xml
<key>CFBundleURLTypes</key>
<array>
    <dict>
        <key>CFBundleURLSchemes</key>
        <array>
            <string>voicecode</string>
        </array>
    </dict>
</array>
```

SwiftUI URL handling:
```swift
.onOpenURL { url in
    if url.scheme == "voicecode", url.host == "session" {
        let sessionId = url.lastPathComponent
        // Set selectedSessionId
    }
}
```

## Verification

title: Add RecordingIndicator visual feedback for push-to-talk
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/design/macos-desktop-redesign.md#2-push-to-talk-voice-input

## Context
When push-to-talk is active, users need clear visual feedback that recording is in progress. This indicator shows a pulsing red dot with instructional text.

## Requirements
- [ ] Create RecordingIndicator view component
- [ ] Add pulsing red circle animation (opacity 1 to 0.5)
- [ ] Show "Recording... (release Option+Space to stop)" text
- [ ] Background pill with red tint
- [ ] Trigger pulseAnimation on appear
- [ ] Only render when voiceInput.isRecording is true
- [ ] Platform-guard with #if os(macOS)

## Technical Approach
- Files to modify: ios/VoiceCode/Utils/PushToTalkModifier.swift (add RecordingIndicator there)
  OR create separate file
- Dependencies: PushToTalkModifier should exist

Key implementation:
```swift
@State private var pulseAnimation = false

Circle()
    .opacity(pulseAnimation ? 1 : 0.5)
    .animation(.easeInOut(duration: 0.5).repeatForever(), value: pulseAnimation)
    .onAppear { pulseAnimation = true }
```

## Verification
- [ ] Manual: Red pulsing dot appears when recording
- [ ] Manual: Instructional text is readable
- [ ] Manual: Indicator disappears when recording stops
- [ ] Visual: Animation is smooth, not jarring

## Acceptance Criteria
- Voice input works seamlessly via both click and keyboard (visual feedback component)

title: Implement PushToTalkModifier for Option+Space voice input
description:
## Design Reference
@docs/design/macos-desktop-redesign.md#2-push-to-talk-voice-input

## Context
Desktop users want hands-on-keyboard voice access. This modifier enables push-to-talk via Option+Space, starting recording on key down and stopping on key up.

## Requirements
- [ ] Create PushToTalkModifier.swift with ViewModifier protocol
- [ ] Accept VoiceInputManager as @ObservedObject parameter
- [ ] Track isHolding state to prevent repeated key events
- [ ] Handle .down phase: start recording if not already holding
- [ ] Handle .up phase: stop recording if was holding
- [ ] Ignore events without Option modifier
- [ ] Create View extension: .pushToTalk(voiceInput:)
- [ ] Platform-guard with #if os(macOS)

## Technical Approach
- Files to create: ios/VoiceCode/Utils/PushToTalkModifier.swift
- Files to modify: ios/VoiceCode/Views/ConversationView.swift
- Dependencies: MacOSRootView should exist for context

Key implementation:
```swift
.onKeyPress(" ", phases: [.down, .up]) { press in
    guard press.modifiers == .option else { return .ignored }
    // Handle down/up phases
}
```

ConversationView integration:
```swift
#if os(macOS)
.pushToTalk(voiceInput: voiceInputManager)
#endif
```

## Verification
- [ ] Unit test: Option+Space down calls startRecording(), sets isHolding true
- [ ] Unit test: Option+Space up calls stopRecording(), sets isHolding false
- [ ] Unit test: Space without Option returns .ignored
- [ ] Manual: Hold Option+Space starts recording with visual feedback
- [ ] Manual: Release stops recording and shows transcription

## Acceptance Criteria
- Push-to-talk works with Option+Space anywhere in app
- Voice input works seamlessly via both click and keyboard

## Parallelization

Can be worked alongside: MacSettingsView, CommandPaletteView
